chunks.text: End of Semester Information EECS Instructional Computer Accounts End of Semester Information Spring 2018 Contents: These Accounts Expire Monday May 14 /home/tmp directories will be deleted after June 1 Labs Close Friday May 11; Server Down Times How

1 Labs Close Friday May 11; Server Down Times How do I Verify My Account Status? What happens to the files in an expired account? How Do I Get off the Expired List? What happens to email sent to an expired account? What happens to my Microsoft Imagi

xpired account? What happens to my Microsoft Imagine account? How can I check my grades? These Accounts Expire Monday May 14 at 9am - Instructional class accounts (such as "ee1-xx", "cs9a-zz", etc) - all Instructional non-CS and non-EECS named acco

- all Instructional non-CS and non-EECS named accounts - Instructional named and "cs199" accounts of CS & EECS majors who graduated or left before January 2018 'NAMED' ACCOUNTS THAT WILL EXPIRE 'CLASS' ACCOUNTS THAT WILL NOT EXPIRE To forward

ts for ongoing CS and EECS students will remain active at least until December 2018 without any action by the user. /home/tmp directories will be deleted after June 1, 2018: All files in the /home/tmp directory will be removed. Please read /sh

tmp directory will be removed. Please read /share/b/pub/removable-media.help for information about backing up your files. Instructional Labs Close May 11 2018 Server Down Times - Cardkey access to the Instructional labs will terminate at midnight

the Instructional labs will terminate at midnight on May 11. - Instructional servers may be down at times for maintenance between May 14 and June 18 servers: status between semesters: downtime affects these services: cory.eecs, ashby.cs, cedar.cs

cts these services: cory.eecs, ashby.cs, cedar.cs on-line ssh logins; Ubuntu Linux hpse-{9,10,15}.eecs on-line ssh LINUX logins; Cadence, Synopsys wprint, iprint on-line print servers license_srv on-line license servers imail on-line instructional em

on-line instructional Windows home dirs cedar, hearst, ward, solano off-line ssh logins; Ubuntu Linux bcom*.eecs, hpse-{11,12,13,14,16}.eecs off-line ssh LINUX logins; Cadence, Synopsys icluster* off-line ssh LINUX logins; Hadoop, MapReduce, Synopsy

-line ssh LINUX logins; Hadoop, MapReduce, Synopsys Isvn off-line Isvn server for classes WSERVER[2-9] off-line Windows terminal server logins Here is the notice we will post by the labs. How do I Verify My Account Status? To verify your account stat

ify My Account Status? To verify your account status, login to WebAcct . How do I Get off the Expired List? If our records are in error, you may complete an EECS Account Request Form and submit it at 378 Cory before May 14. Or you can just wait u

378 Cory before May 14. Or you can just wait until the start of the next semester, when we will have newer enrollment records, and request your account again . What happens to the files in an expired account? For class accounts ("cs61a-aa", "e

xpired account? For class accounts ("cs61a-aa", "ee122-aa", etc), the files will be saved for 1-2 weeks, archived to tape and deleted. Class account logins are reassigned to new students next semester. For named accounts ("jdoe", 'cs199-aa'

emester. For named accounts ("jdoe", 'cs199-aa', etc), the files will be saved for about a month after the start of the next semester, and the files will be archived to tape before they are deleted. If the student is eligible again for a

eleted. If the student is eligible again for an account next semester, the account can be renewed . BEFORE your account expires, you can copy your files from your Windows and UNIX home directories by transferring them to a USB memory stick.

tories by transferring them to a USB memory stick. You could also email them to yourself or use a file transfer program. AFTER your account expires, you will not be able to copy the files. For more information, please see - How Do I Copy Fi

more information, please see - How Do I Copy Files Between Computers? - http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/cgi-bin/pub.cgi?file=file-transfer.help What happens to email sent to an expired account? The files in an expired class account are removed from

iles in an expired class account are removed from our disk a week or two after the end of the semester. The files for an expired named account remain on the disk until about 1 month into the next semester. BEFORE and AFTER your account expi

semester. BEFORE and AFTER your account expires (as long as the account still exists), you can download email from imail.eecs.berkeley.edu, the Instructional IMAP server. AFTER your account expires, no new email is accepted here.

count expires, no new email is accepted here. For more information, please see - How Do I Manage Email? - http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/connecting.html#email Email sent to an expired account can only be forwarded to another system - it wi

can only be forwarded to another system - it will not be saved here. You can also have your own automated reply that tells the sender about your new email address. If you do not set this up, the senders will receive an automated reply telli

the senders will receive an automated reply telling them that the email address is no longer valid. Email will be forwarded for up to one year after a named account expires. These features are set in a .procmailrc file in your UNIX home di

are set in a .procmailrc file in your UNIX home directory. You can create or edit the file using a text editor (vi, emacs) when logged into your UNIX account. You can also copy a .procmailrc into an expired account using 'sftp' (see What hap

o an expired account using 'sftp' (see What happens to the files in an expired account? ). Each rule in .procmailrc starts with a ":0" line. The "c" in :0c: says to continue to the next rule after processing this one. To enable email forwa

fter processing this one. To enable email forwarding, add a rule like this at the beginning of the rules in .procmailrc: :0c: ! me@new.edu To enable an auto-reply that is sent in response to incoming email, add this rule at the beginnin

to incoming email, add this rule at the beginning of the rules in .procmailrc: :0c: * !^From[: ].*$LOGNAME | /usr/bin/vacation $LOGNAME For auto-replies, you also need to create a message in a file called .vacation.msg and initialize the

a file called .vacation.msg and initialize the UNIX "vacation" program. For example: echo "My new email address is me@new.edu." > ~/.vacation.msg vacation -I You can use a editor to create a better message in the .vacation.msg f

to create a better message in the .vacation.msg file. See "man vacation" on a UNIX system for more options. How can I check my grades? The Registrar posts grades at CalCentral . If your class used the EECS Instructional grading software (' s

used the EECS Instructional grading software (' submit ' and ' glookup '), you can still see the 'glookup' results after the account has expired: Login at the WebAcct site: http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/webacct/ . Select "List local grade

erkeley.edu/webacct/ . Select "List local grades - glookup". Your grades will be displayed for any EECS classes that use a local grading database ('glookup') this semester. This lasts for 1-2 weeks after the account has expired. What hap

2 weeks after the account has expired. What happens to my Microsoft Imagine account? All Spring 2018 EECS Microsoft Imagine (formerly called "Dreamspark" or "e-Academy") accounts will expire at 9am on May 14, 2018. The EECS Microsoft Imagine

9am on May 14, 2018. The EECS Microsoft Imagine site will be unavailable from May 14 - June 18 2018. Students who are eligible after Jun 18 2018 can request or renew a Microsoft Imagine account by following the instructions on http://msdnaa.

nt by following the instructions on http://msdnaa.eecs.berkeley.edu/ . 'CLASS' ACCOUNTS THAT WILL NOT EXPIRE Instructors must notify inst@eecs about any class accounts that need to be extended after May 14. ee105-aaf (expires 9am May 21) cs161-a

ay 14. ee105-aaf (expires 9am May 21) cs161-aqa (expires 9am May 21) cs164-ace (expires 9am May 21) 'NAMED' ACCOUNTS THAT WILL EXPIRE Users can use WebAcct to check on the status of their Instructional UNIX named accounts: login using your

uctional UNIX named accounts: login using your CalNet ID or Instructional UNIX account. alspaugh david.schinazi dunn hokeunkim jlkramer j.sadie nihar samsmith suchitb t.b wangd wjs10 xinghao cs199-fy cs199-fz cs199-hd cs199-im
